cross_review_notes: The performance-review's own diag pull for this report (prior-window trades/closed, limit=500) hit the EXACT SAME query-timeout bug performance-review flagged in anomalies — independently reproduced by the master-review session, raising confidence this is a real, systemic issue, not a fluke.

ETH rallied +5.0% and BTC +3.3% over the window (see market_context) — directly contextualizes why eth_pullback_2h (a mean-reversion/pullback strategy) was the single largest paper loser this window (-$1439.96 total): a strong directional rally is the worst regime for a countertrend pullback entry. This mirrors the paper-book-tracker's broader finding that the pullback_2h family (not trend_4h) is the persistent drag.

Both ml-review's fc-pcv frozen-dataset finding and performance-review's paper-book decay finding trace to the same underlying pattern: stale/frozen training data (ML) and un-refreshed cohort tracking (paper-book) both went unnoticed for 2-3 weeks because nothing was actively re-verifying them each run — until this session's mandatory soak-integrity audit and the tracker bug-fix respectively surfaced both. Recommend a standing 'freshness self-check' habit across both domains, not just this one-time catch.
